title: Query major incidents opened in the last 24 hours
question: Write a background script that lists major incidents opened in the last 24 hours and prints the number, business service, and state.
summary: Useful interview script for major-incident review and reporting.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord on incident, filter the major incident flag and sys_created_on for the last 24 hours, then print the relevant fields.
steps: Open System Definition > Scripts - Background.
Paste the script.
Run it in a non-production-safe context.
Review output for the major incident list.
commonMistakes: Filtering only on priority = 1 instead of the actual major incident field/process indicator.
Using hoursAgoStart incorrectly in client-side code.
explanation: This script gives operational visibility into recent major incidents without building a report first.

title: Flag repeat incidents for problem candidate review
question: Write a script include method that counts incidents with the same configuration item in the last 7 days and returns true when the threshold is met.
summary: Shows a common pattern for incident trend to problem-candidate logic.
exactAnswer: Use GlideAggregate on incident, constrain by CI and opened date, and compare the count to the threshold.
steps: Create a new Script Include.
Paste the method.
Call it from a business rule or flow action wrapper as needed.
expectedOutput: Returns true when incident volume for the CI meets or exceeds the threshold.
commonMistakes: Using GlideRecord and counting manually when GlideAggregate is cleaner.
Not validating threshold as an integer.

title: Prevent conflicting change windows
question: Write a before insert/update business rule snippet that blocks a normal change if another overlapping scheduled change exists for the same CI.
exactAnswer: Query change_request for overlapping planned windows on the same CI and abort the action when a qualifying conflicting change is found.
steps: Create a before business rule on change_request.
Run only for relevant change types and states.
Abort with a clear message when overlap is detected.
expectedOutput: Insert or update is blocked when an overlapping active change exists.
commonMistakes: Checking only exact same start time instead of overlap logic.
Forgetting to exclude the current record on update.

title: Backfill owned_by on business applications
question: Write a fix script that sets owned_by from the support group manager when owned_by is empty on business applications.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord on cmdb_ci_business_app, check for blank owned_by, derive the support_group manager, and update carefully.
steps: Open System Definition > Fix Scripts.
Paste the script and validate in a lower environment first.
Run only after confirming the support_group relationship is populated consistently.
expectedOutput: Business application records without owned_by are backfilled from the support group manager.
commonMistakes: Running mass update without verifying support group quality.
Not considering update business rules that may fire.

title: List duplicate CI names by class
question: Write a background script that prints duplicate CI names within the same class.
exactAnswer: Use GlideAggregate grouped by sys_class_name and name, aggregate count, and print only groups with a count above one.
steps: Open Scripts - Background.
Paste the GlideAggregate script.
Review the results before deciding a cleanup action.
commonMistakes: Treating same-name records across different classes as duplicates automatically.
Cleaning duplicates without checking identification rules and source precedence.

title: Find recent MID Server ECC errors
question: Write a background script that lists ECC Queue error records for a specific MID Server from the last 2 days.
exactAnswer: Filter ecc_queue by agent, recent sys_created_on, and error state or error-like response patterns, then print the payload summary.
steps: Open Scripts - Background.
Replace the MID Server name in the script.
Run and inspect recent error rows.
commonMistakes: Not filtering by MID Server agent.
Assuming every failed discovery creates state=error without checking queue patterns.

title: Route vulnerabilities to a remediation group
question: Write a business rule snippet that assigns a vulnerability group based on the CI support group when the vulnerability item is created.
summary: Maps ownership from CI to vulnerability handling.
exactAnswer: Look up the vulnerable item CI, read its support group, and populate the vulnerability assignment field or group field in a before business rule.
steps: Create a before insert business rule on the vulnerable item table.
Resolve the related CI.
Set the vulnerability group field from the CI ownership model.
expectedOutput: New vulnerable items inherit the support group from the CI when available.
commonMistakes: Ignoring cases where CI ownership is missing.
Hardcoding assignment groups instead of using ownership data.

title: Count open security incidents by state
question: Write a background script that prints the count of open security incidents by state.
exactAnswer: Use GlideAggregate on the security incident table, group by state, and print the state and count.
steps: Open Scripts - Background.
Use the security incident table in your instance.
Run and review grouped counts.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong table name for the installed SecOps app version.
Forgetting that display values may differ from numeric states.

title: Transfer an asset between stockrooms in script logic
question: Write a server-side script snippet that updates an asset stockroom and location while recording a work note.
exactAnswer: Retrieve the asset, update stockroom and location fields, add work notes, and save the record with appropriate governance.
steps: Use the script in a fix script, background script, or controlled server logic.
Validate that the destination stockroom and location are correct.
expectedOutput: Selected asset record reflects the new stockroom and location.
commonMistakes: Updating stockroom without updating operational location where required.
Moving assets without an approval or inventory process context.

title: List software install records that still need normalization review
question: Write a script that prints software install evidence with missing or weak normalized product mapping.
summary: Targets SAM cleanup and compliance readiness.
exactAnswer: Query software install evidence that lacks the expected normalized product or version references and print the raw publisher/product details for review.
steps: Confirm the install evidence table name in the target environment.
Run the script in a safe environment.
Use the output to drive normalization worklist review.
commonMistakes: Assuming field names are identical in every SAM setup.
Treating every unmapped install as a licensing issue before normalization review.

title: Calculate aging vulnerabilities older than 30 days
question: Write a script that prints open vulnerable items older than 30 days with assignment group and CI.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord on vulnerable items, filter active/open records with opened date older than 30 days, and print ownership context for remediation review.
steps: Open Scripts - Background.
Run the query and export results if needed.
commonMistakes: Using created date when the process expects opened date or first-seen date.

title: Demonstrate GlideRecordSecure for user-respecting queries
question: Write a script include method that returns incident numbers using GlideRecordSecure so ACLs are respected for the current user context.
summary: Shows secure query design for interview discussion.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecordSecure instead of GlideRecord when the returned records must respect the current user ACL access.
steps: Create a Script Include intended for server-side or Ajax-backed use.
Return only necessary data.
expectedOutput: Returns only incident numbers visible to the current user context.
commonMistakes: Using GlideRecord when user-respecting data retrieval is required.
Returning more fields than necessary.

title: Build an encoded query for stale CIs
question: Write a background script that finds servers not updated in 90 days using an encoded query and prints name and last discovered date.
summary: Tests encoded query fluency with CMDB hygiene.
exactAnswer: Use addEncodedQuery with a stale last_discovered condition, then print the records for review.
steps: Open Scripts - Background.
Paste the script and verify field names.
commonMistakes: Using an encoded query against the wrong class table.
Assuming install_status values without checking the instance choices.

title: Summarize high-risk vendors
question: Write a background script that prints vendor risk records above a defined score threshold with owner and status.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord on the vendor-risk assessment table used in the environment, filter score above threshold, and print ownership context.
steps: Confirm the target table in the environment.
Set the threshold value.
Run and validate output.
expectedOutput: Acme Hosting Assessment | Priya Patel | In Review
commonMistakes: Using assumed table names without confirming the implementation.
Treating score fields as universal across every GRC setup.

title: Create a known-error style note from a problem record
question: Write a server-side script snippet that copies workaround information from a problem record into a knowledge draft or related note trigger point.
exactAnswer: Query the problem record, validate that a workaround exists, and create a knowledge draft or downstream action with the relevant text.
steps: Retrieve the problem record.
Validate workaround text.
Insert a knowledge draft or call a dedicated utility.
expectedOutput: A draft knowledge article is created with the workaround content.
commonMistakes: Publishing automatically without review governance.
Not validating knowledge base and ownership requirements.
